title: Let touch drags scroll the terminal (xterm was eating touchmove)
action: Add pointer-events:none to .term-viewport .xterm (and children) so xterm's touchmove/preventDefault handler no longer swallows the swipe; the touch then reaches .term-viewport which scrolls natively. The screen is read-only (disableStdin, driven by the bar buttons + d-pad) so disabling pointer interaction on it is safe.

truths: Touch/swipe over the terminal scrolls .term-viewport instead of being cancelled by xterm
Bar buttons, d-pad and reply still work (they are outside .xterm)
artifacts: styles.css rule pointer-events:none on the xterm content
prohibitions: Do not alter the poll/applyScreen logic

verify: cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: pointer-events:none on the read-only xterm so touch swipes scroll .term-viewport natively instead of being cancelled by xterm's touchmove handler
trace.verify_output: bundle ok; vitest 20 passed; cargo test + clippy clean. Headless: .xterm computed pointer-events=none, .term-viewport still canScroll (115->0 programmatic), Keys button opens pad and d-pad Down still sends <down>.
trace.verify_command: cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run
